paper_title: Meteoric Isotopic and Meteorological Data Analysis in Northern Eurasia

methods.description: This method involves collecting and analyzing stable isotopic observation data from various stations to perform statistical analysis and determine local meteoric water lines.

steps.description: The δ 18 O p data were downloaded through the IAEA website.
steps.searchable_summary: download δ 18 O p data from IAEA website

steps.description: We used an ordinary least square regression (OLSR) to determine the local meteoric water line (LMWL), which is of the form δ 2 H = a δ 18 O + b.

methods.description: This method involves collecting meteorological data such as NAOI and EZCI from various sources for further analysis.

steps.description: The NAOI data were downloaded from the National Centers for Environmental Prediction (NCEP) of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
steps.searchable_summary: download NAOI data from NOAA NCEP

steps.description: The Eurasian zonal circulation index (EZCI) data were downloaded from the National Climate Center of the China Meteorological Administration.
steps.searchable_summary: download EZCI data from China Meteorological Administration

methods.description: This method estimates moisture transportation paths using NCEP reanalysis data and HYSPLIT back-trajectory analysis.

steps.description: NCEP reanalysis data sets were used as the meteorological input for HYSPLIT back-trajectory analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch NCEP reanalysis data for HYSPLIT

steps.description: 240-h (10-day) back trajectories were calculated for target sites at variable heights 500, 1000, 1500, 2500 m above ground level (a.g.l.) using HYSPLIT.
Statistical clustering of similar trajectories was done through the internal HYSPLIT clustering program.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical clustering on trajectory data
